plan: 1. Install ESLint and TypeScript ESLint dependencies: `npm install -D eslint @eslint/js typescript-eslint globals`

2. Create `eslint.config.js` (flat config format) with TypeScript support — configure recommended rules from @eslint/js and typescript-eslint, set parser to typescript-eslint, target src/**/*.ts files, and include browser globals
3. Add `lint` script to package.json: `"lint": "eslint src/"` — also add `"test": "vitest run"` script (vitest is already installed but has no run script)
4. Create `vitest.config.ts` with basic configuration — import from vitest/config, set include pattern to `tests/**/*.test.ts`, and configure any needed settings (environment: 'node' for unit tests)
5. Update `tsconfig.json` include array to also cover `tests/**/*.ts` and config files (`*.config.ts`) so typecheck covers tests and configs
6. Create a sample unit test at `tests/us-002/sample.test.ts` that exercises a simple pure function to satisfy the 'a sample unit test passes' acceptance criterion
7. Verify all three scripts pass: run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` — fix any issues that arise
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run lint` and verify it exits 0 with no errors on existing src/ code
Run `npm run typecheck` and verify it exits 0 (already exists but confirm it still works after tsconfig include changes)
Run `npm run test` and verify the sample unit test plus existing US-001 tests all pass
Intentionally introduce a lint error (e.g., unused variable) to confirm ESLint catches it
Intentionally introduce a type error to confirm typecheck catches it
risks: TypeScript ESLint version compatibility — ensure the installed typescript-eslint version supports TypeScript ^6.0.3
Existing US-001 tests may fail under the new vitest config if include/exclude patterns don't match their location
Flat config ESLint format requires eslint v9+ — verify the installed version supports it
Changing tsconfig include to cover tests/ may surface type errors in existing test files
